# AI Necklace Virtual Try On

## Overview
Luxurious Look and Feel with State-of-the-Art Virtual Try-On for Necklace
Precise AI neck and clavicle tracking gives users an ultra-realistic AR try-on experience, recreating the luxurious look and feel of physical necklace sampling.

Create realistic and dynamic necklace vitual try-on from a 2D image, no expensive 3D modelling required. Our advanced algorithms create lifelike virtual try-on necklace SKUs with sophisticated lighting effects and physically accurate motions.

### Integration Guide
This guide walks you through:

*   **Endpoint:** `/s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/necklace`
*   **Authentication:** All requests require an `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`
*   **Workflow:**
    1.  **Prepare a selfie image:** Uploading an image or provide a valid image URL
    2.  **Prepare a necklace image:** Uploading an image or provide a valid image URL of a necklace product
    3.  **Fire an AI task and Retrieve Task ID:** Capture the `task_id` from the response.
    4.  **Poll Status (`GET`):** Use the `task_id` to check the status of the task. Continue polling until `task_status` is `"success"` or `"error"`.

---

* API Playground

Interactively explore and test the API using our official playground:

**API Playground:**
[http://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-playground/ai-necklace-virtual-try-on/](http://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-playground/ai-necklace-virtual-try-on/)

---

* Authentication
- Include your API key in the request header using **Bearer Token**:
    ```
    Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
    ```
You can find your API Key at https://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-keys/.


* 1. Upload an Image

You may upload a file directly to the server or provide a valid image URL in the VTO task payload.

   * Upload Endpoint

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/necklace
```

Alternatively, skip this step if you already have a public image URL.

You may upload a file directly to the URL provided in the response from the File API and then use the corresponding `src_file_id` returned by the File API to invoke the AI task later. Or provide a valid image URL in the VTO task payload as `src_file_url`. The `src_file_id` or `src_file_url` will serve as the virtual try-on target.

You must also provide another necklace product image as a reference using `ref_file_ids` or `ref_file_urls` to be applied to your `src_file_id` or `src_file_url`.

The AI engine supports automatic background removal for your selfie. However, you may provide an occlusion mask image file for your neck (`srcmsk_file_id` or `srcmsk_file_url`) to fine-tune the segmentation.

---

* 2. Create a Necklace VTO Task and Poll for Results

Once you have an image and a template ID, create a task. The API processes the request asynchronously. You must poll the task status until it reaches `success` or `error`.

   * Create Task Endpoint

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/necklace
```

   * Polling Endpoint

```
GET /s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/necklace/{task_id}
```

---

### File Specs & Errors

* AI Necklace Virtual Try-On Specification

**Supported Necklace View**
A front-facing image of the necklace worn, with the background removed.

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/necklace_product_01_124206cfbe_3993a2128d.jpg)

**Supported Selfie View**
A front-facing selfie with the neck clearly visible and unobstructed. Horizontal head rotation is supported within 20 degrees. The head size should be proportionate, and the neck width should occupy at least 15 per cent of the image width.

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/Necklace_restriction_83410fb6c1.png)

**necklace\_wearing\_location: array of two points (optional)**
Specifies the target locations in the photo where the necklace should be placed.
Default: null (engine default)

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/wearing_location_874264bb70.jpg)

**necklace\_shadow\_intensity: float (0.0 to 1.0)**
Controls the shadow strength:
0.0 represents no shadow
1.0 represents maximum shadow
Default value: 0.15

**necklace\_ambient\_light\_intensity: float (0.0 to 1.0)**
Defines how much the lighting references the selfie image:
0.0 ignores the selfie image lighting
1.0 fully matches the selfie image lighting and shadow rendering
Default value: 1.0

**necklace\_anchor\_point: array of two points in pixel coordinate (optional)**
Specifies the anchor points for the left and right visible ends of the necklace chain in the product image, used for alignment.
Default: null (engine default)

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/anchor_point_7f9b254ca4.jpg)

---

* Supported Formats & Dimensions

|AI Feature|Supported Dimensions|Supported File Size|Supported Formats|
|  ----  | ----  | ----  | ----  |
|AI Necklace Virtual Try-On|long side <= 4096 |< 10MB|jpg/jpeg/png|

* Error Codes

|Error Code|Description|
|  ----  | ----  |
| RUNTIME_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred dunecklace runtime |
| PHOTO_DETECTION_FAIL | The user photo could not be processed correctly, for example no neck detected |
| OBJECT_DETECTION_FAIL | The object photo could not be processed correctly, for example no product detected |
| PHOTO_CHECK_INVALID | The pose or size of the user photo is invalid |
| INPUT_ERROR | The input file format is incorrect |
| INPUT_MAIN_IMAGE_EMPTY | A user image is required |


* Environment & Dependency

| Sample Code Language / Tool | Recommended Runtime Versions |
|---|---|
| cURL | - bash >= 3.2</br>   - curl >= 7.58 (modern TLS/HTTP support)</br>   - jq >= 1.6 (robust JSON parsing) |
| Node.js (JavaScript) | Node >= 18 (for global fetch) |
| JavaScript | - Chrome / Edge >= 80</br>   - Firefox >= 74</br>   - Safari >= 13.1 |
| PHP | PHP >= 7.4 (for modern TLS/compat), ext-curl (recommended) or allow_url_fopen=On + ext-openssl, ext-json |
| Python | Python >= 3.10 (for f-strings), requests >= 2.20.0 |
| Java | Java 11+ (for HttpClient), Jackson Databind >= 2.12.0 |

## Endpoints

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/necklace`
**Create a new file.**

To upload a new file, you'll first need to use the File API. It will give you a URL – use that URL to upload your file. Once the upload is finished, you can use the file_id from the same response to start using our AI features.

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `FileV1.1`

This object represents a file.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | Yes | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | Yes | Content MIME type for this file. Currently available values are listed in the enum. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of this file |
| `files.file_size` | `integer` | Yes | Content length for this file in bytes. Should not be larger than 10MB. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful retrieval of file upload information

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicFileResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | No | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | No | Content MIME type for this file. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | No | Name of this file |
| `files.file_id` | `string` | No | ID of this file. Other run task API will need this \`file_id\`. |
| `files.requests` | `array<object>` | No | Using upload \`url\`, \`headers\`, \`method\` below to upload file. After completion, the \`file_id\` is used to proceed with calling run task API. |
| `files.requests.headers` | `object` | No | Headers to include when uploading the file |
| `files.requests.url` | `string` | No | URL to upload this file |
| `files.requests.method` | `string` | No | HTTP method to upload this file |

**400** — Invalid request parameters

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
| `error_code` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/necklace`
**Run an AI 2D Virtual Try On Necklace task.**

This endpoint initiates the necklace virtual try-on process. You must provide source file(s) and reference image(s) (via URL or File ID), along with specific parameters for alignment, shadowing, and wearing location. The task will be processed asynchronously, and you can check its status using the task_id returned in this response.

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileUrls | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileIds | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileUrls | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileIds & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcInfo & object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `src_file_url` | `string` | Yes | Url of the file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `srcmsk_file_url` | `string` | No | Url of the source mask file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `ref_file_urls` | `array<string>` | Yes | Url of the reference file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `ref_file_ids` | `array<string>` | Yes | ID of the reference file file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `src_file_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `srcmsk_file_id` | `string` | No | ID of the source mask file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `source_info` | `object` | Yes | Contain source photo informations. |
| `source_info.name` | `string` | Yes | The source url or File ID. |
| `source_info.mask_name` | `string` | No | The source mask url or File ID. |
| `object_infos` | `array<object>` | Yes | — |
| `object_infos.name` | `string` | Yes | The reference url or File ID. Indicate which reference product image should be matched. |
| `object_infos.parameter` | `object` | No | Contain several object informations in this list. |
| `object_infos.parameter.necklace_need_remove_background` | `boolean` | No | Whether the necklace image needs background removal. |
| `object_infos.parameter.necklace_anchor_point` | `array<array<integer>>` | No | The anchor points of the necklace image, used for alignment. <br>Type: 2x2 list of integers <br>Range: [[x, y], [x, y]] (pixel coordinates) <br>Default: null (use engine default) |
| `object_infos.parameter.necklace_wearing_location` | `array<array<integer>>` | No | The target locations of photo where the necklace should be placed. <br>Type: 2x2 list of integers <br>Range: [[x, y], [x, y]] (pixel coordinates) <br>Default: null (use engine default) |
| `object_infos.parameter.necklace_shadow_intensity` | `number` | No | Shadow intensity applied to the necklace (higher = darker shadow). |
| `object_infos.parameter.necklace_ambient_light_intensity` | `number` | No | Ambient light adjustment intensity for the necklace. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful execution of the task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicRunTaskResponseV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `data` | `object` | No | — |
| `data.task_id` | `string` | No | ID of this task. Task result is valid to query by this ID for 24 hours. |

**400** — Failed execution of task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `RunError`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | Error message |
| `error_code` | `string — InvalidParameters \| CreditInsufficiency \| InvalidStyleGroup \| InvalidStyle \| BadRequest` | No | Error code:   * InvalidParameters - Invalid request parameters   * CreditInsufficiency - Insufficient unit to run   * BadRequest - Unexpected request parameter   * InvalidStyleGroup - Invalid style group id   * InvalidStyle - Invalid style id |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**429** — Too many requests

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `GET` `/s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/necklace/{task_id}`
**Check the status of a AI 2D Virtual Try On Necklace task.**

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Parameters

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `task_id` | path | `string` | Yes | ID of task to check |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful check of the task status

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `TaskStatusResponseBodySingleUrlResultsV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `string` | No | URL to download this result. Valid for 2 hours |

**400** — Invalid task ID

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**500** — Task execution timeout

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
